Here is a vision, beginning at [reference], and continued to the end of the book, which is rightly considered to be one of the most difficult portions in all the book of God. When we despair of being satisfied about any difficulty we encounter, let us bless God that our salvation does not depend on it, but that necessary things are plain enough; and let us wait until God shall reveal even this unto us. This chapter describes two outer courts of the temple. Whether the person mentioned here was the Son of God or a created angel is not clear. But Christ is both our Altar and our Sacrifice, to whom we must look with faith in all approaches to God; and he is Salvation in the midst of the earth, to be looked unto from all directions.
